title: Social Square Match Market
topic: consent-aware introductions, events, and community requests
skill_description: Aggregate needs, offers, consent state, and event signals into reviewable social match proposals.
review_reason: keeper reviews consent, fit, asymmetry, risk, and follow-up plan before introductions
quality_rules: No external introduction can bypass consent review.
Each match must explain mutual value and uncertainty.
Every output must include a low-risk next interaction or ask-for-consent step.
skill_rules: Aggregate needs, offers, constraints, and trust signals before matching.
Prefer small reversible interactions over broad public exposure.

participants.title: Consent Ledger Desk
participants.perspective: Matching quality depends on explicit consent and scoped disclosure.
participants.current_question: Which introduction requires consent before any action?
participants.contribution: Provides allowed contact scope, blocked topics, and review flags.

participants.title: Community Requests Desk
participants.perspective: Weak-tie value comes from matching concrete needs and offers, not generic affinity.
participants.current_question: Which need/offer pair creates useful but low-risk contact?
participants.contribution: Provides request summaries, offer summaries, constraints, and possible next actions.

participants.title: Event Loop Desk
participants.perspective: Events are social protocols that need invitation, reminder, and outcome records.
participants.current_question: Which match should become a small event rather than a direct introduction?
participants.contribution: Provides event context, RSVP state, and follow-up capture plan.
